---
title: "From pixels to patterns — results and visualisation"
author: "Emma Belaud"
format:
  html:
    css: css/styles.css
    embed-resources: true
    code-tools: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

# Overview

## Role in the pipeline

This is the **final step** of the SEM pipeline, following:

1.  `1_database_edition.qmd` — assembles the SEM database
2.  `2_SEM_diagnostic.qmd` — selects window duration, transformation, and valid windows
3.  `3_SEM_modelisation.qmd` — fits all models and writes result databases

**No modelling is performed here.** Every number in every figure comes directly from pre-computed CSV files. Re-running this script is fast and safe — it never touches a model.

## Input databases

| File | What it contains |
|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| `H1_adjusted_values.csv` | Per-observation null-model residuals with date, land-use label, season |
| `H1_model_summary.csv` | β, SE, t, p, Hedge's g, R² from the two-step H1 model |
| `DAG_coefficients.csv` | Path coefficients from the full-dataset piecewise SEM |
| `DAG_model_fit.csv` | Fisher's C, AIC for the full-dataset piecewise SEM |
| `SEM_results_database.csv` | Rolling-window path coefficients for all taxa |
| `indirect_effects.csv` | Rolling-window indirect effect products for all taxa |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r setup}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETUP
#
# Loads packages, reads shared pipeline metadata (microclimate variable names),
# and defines all visual constants and helper functions used across sections
# H1, H2, and H3. All aesthetic choices (colours, linetypes, labels) are
# centralised here so that changing one value propagates consistently.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))    # Force English month names in plots
dir.create("../output", showWarnings = FALSE)

libs <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "patchwork",
          "kableExtra", "colorspace", "nlme")
invisible(lapply(libs, require, character.only = TRUE))


# ── Microclimate variable names ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Written by 1_database_edition.qmd via PCA-based index selection.
# Reading them here ensures axis labels always match the actual data columns
# (e.g. "temp_soil_amplitude", "hum_soil_amplitude").
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")
mc1_pred <- microclimate_index[1]
mc2_pred <- microclimate_index[2]


# ── Land-use colour palette ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Dark green = treed plots (A); burnt orange = cultivated plots (C).
# Applied consistently to every figure that distinguishes land-use type.
pos_colours <- c("Treed (A)" = "#717F55", "Cultivated (C)" = "#FF994E")


# ── Causal pathway metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Single source of truth for pathway labels, colours, and linetypes.
# Used to populate ggplot scale_*_manual() calls throughout H2 and H3.
#
# Columns:
#   path_id  — machine key used as join key in all downstream code
#   label    — human-readable string shown in legends and on axes
#   colour   — warm tones for root-mediated paths; cool tones for microclimate
#   ltype    — encodes causal depth:
#                solid  = direct effect or composite (total indirect / total effect)
#                dashed = two-step indirect   (lu → X → fauna)
#                dotted = three-step indirect (lu → X → root → fauna)
path_meta <- tribble(
  ~path_id,         ~label,                                                 ~colour,   ~ltype,
  "direct",         "Direct (lu\u2192fauna)",                               "#F9BB05", "solid",
  "via_mc1",        paste0("lu\u2192", mc1_pred, "\u2192fauna"),            "#BCD094", "solid",
  "via_mc2",        paste0("lu\u2192", mc2_pred, "\u2192fauna"),            "#717F55", "solid",
  "via_root",       "lu\u2192root\u2192fauna",                              "#C45300", "solid",
  "via_mc1_root",   paste0("lu\u2192", mc1_pred, "\u2192root\u2192fauna"),  "#FF7E1F", "solid",
  "via_mc2_root",   paste0("lu\u2192", mc2_pred, "\u2192root\u2192fauna"),  "#FF994E", "solid",
  "total_indirect", "Total indirect",                                        "#999999", "solid",
  "total_effect",   "Total effect",                                          "#444444", "solid"
)

# Named vectors for ggplot scale_*_manual() — names = path_id
path_colours <- setNames(path_meta$colour, path_meta$path_id)
path_ltypes  <- setNames(path_meta$ltype,  path_meta$path_id)
path_labels  <- setNames(path_meta$label,  path_meta$path_id)


# ── Season background bands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Alternating grey rectangles and italic labels mark the four meteorological
# seasons. Winter is treated as a cross-year band (Dec–Feb).
season_order <- c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter")
season_fills <- c(Spring = "#f9f9f9", Summer = "#eeeeee",
                  Autumn = "#f9f9f9", Winter = "#eeeeee")


# ── Agricultural intervention landmarks ──────────────────────────────────────
# Stored as a data frame so geom_vline() can reference it directly.
# Dotted dark-red vertical lines are added to all temporal panels.
interventions <- data.frame(
  date = as.Date(c(
    "2024-07-08", "2024-07-15",
    "2024-11-22", "2024-11-25", 
    "2025-03-14", "2025-07-10"
  )),
  event = c(
    "Wheat Harvest", "Sorghum Sown",
    "Sorghum Mown",  "Tilled",       
    "Chickpea Sown", "Chickpea Harvest"
  )
)

irrigation <- data.frame(
  date = as.Date(c(
    "2024-07-17", "2024-07-22",
    "2024-08-12"
  )))


# ── Helper: season annotation layers ─────────────────────────────────────────
#' Build season background rectangles and italic season labels for a time plot.
#'
#' Returns a list of ggplot layers (annotate + geom_text) ready for `+`
#' concatenation onto any plot with a date-scaled x-axis.
#'
#' Winter is handled as a cross-year band: xmax uses year + 1 when
#' m_end < m_start. Bands are clipped to `date_range` so they never exceed
#' the actual data extent.
#'
#' @param date_range  Length-2 Date vector `c(start, end)` matching the x-axis.
#' @param y_label_pos Vertical position of season name labels (default = Inf,
#'                    i.e. top of panel).
#' @param label_size  ggplot font size for season labels. Default 2.2.
#' @return A list of ggplot annotation / geom layers.
make_season_layers <- function(date_range, y_label_pos = Inf, label_size = 2.2) {

  bands <- tibble(
    season  = names(season_fills),
    m_start = c(3L, 6L, 9L, 12L),
    m_end   = c(6L, 9L, 12L,  3L),
    fill    = unname(season_fills)
  ) %>%
    crossing(year = seq(year(date_range[1]), year(date_range[2]))) %>%
    mutate(
      xmin = as.Date(paste(year, m_start, "01", sep = "-")),
      xmax = as.Date(paste(
        if_else(m_end < m_start, year + 1L, year),
        m_end, "01", sep = "-"
      )),
      xmid = xmin + (xmax - xmin) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(xmin < date_range[2], xmax > date_range[1]) %>%
    mutate(
      xmin = pmax(xmin, date_range[1]),
      xmax = pmin(xmax, date_range[2])
    )

  rect_layers <- pmap(
    bands %>% select(xmin, xmax, fill),
    \(xmin, xmax, fill) annotate(
      "rect",
      xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
      fill = fill, alpha = 1
    )
  )

  label_layer <- geom_text(
    data        = bands,
    aes(x = xmid, y = y_label_pos, label = season),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = 1.6, size = label_size,
    colour = "grey60", fontface = "italic", family = "sans"
  )

  c(rect_layers, list(label_layer))
}


# ── Base ggplot theme ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#' Consistent theme for all SEM figures.
#'
#' Built on theme_classic() with overrides for panel borders, grids,
#' typography, and legend placement. A single call here guarantees visual
#' consistency across H1, H2, and H3 panels.
#'
#' @param base_size  Base font size in pt. Default 8.
#' @param legend_pos Passed to `legend.position`. Default "none".
#' @return A complete ggplot2 theme object.
theme_sem <- function(base_size = 8, legend_pos = "none") {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) %+replace%
    theme(
      panel.border      = element_rect(colour = "grey20", fill = NA,
                                       linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "grey94", linewidth = 0.25),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
      panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      axis.line         = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks        = element_line(colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.3),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(2, "pt"),
      axis.title        = element_text(size = base_size - 0.5, colour = "grey20"),
      axis.text         = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, colour = "grey30"),
      strip.text        = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                       colour = "grey15"),
      strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "grey96", colour = "grey96",
                                       linewidth = 0.3),
      plot.title        = element_text(size = base_size + 0.5, face = "bold",
                                       hjust = 0, colour = "grey10",
                                       margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = base_size - 1, colour = "grey45",
                                       hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 5)),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = base_size - 2, colour = "grey55",
                                       hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 4)),
      legend.position   = legend_pos,
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.key        = element_blank(),
      legend.title      = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                       colour = "grey20"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, colour = "grey30"),
      legend.key.size   = unit(8, "pt"),
      legend.spacing.x  = unit(3, "pt"),
      plot.margin       = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    )
}


# ── Load pre-computed result databases ────────────────────────────────────────
# All modelling was performed upstream in 3_SEM_modelisation.qmd.
# This script reads outputs only — no model is fitted here.

h1_adj <- read.csv("../output/H1_adjusted_values.csv",
                   na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(
    position_label = factor(position_label,
                            levels = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")),
    season         = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  )

h1_summary <- read.csv("../output/H1_model_summary.csv",
                       na.strings = c("", "NA"))

dag_table <- read.csv("../output/DAG_coefficients.csv",
                      na.strings = c("", "NA"))

dag_fit <- read.csv("../output/DAG_model_fit.csv",
                    na.strings = c("", "NA"))

sem_results <- read.csv("../output/SEM_results_database.csv",
                        na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(season = factor(season, levels = season_order))

indirect_effects <- read.csv("../output/indirect_effects.csv",
                             na.strings = c("", "NA"))

# Faunal transformation used during modelling (e.g. "log", "sqrt_plus_one").
# Written by 3_SEM_modelisation.qmd; used for informative axis labels.
transfo_fauna <- h1_summary$transformation[1]
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. H1 — Direct effect of land-use type on faunal activity

> **Land-use type directly structures faunal activity, with treed habitats sustaining lower but more temporally stable activity than cultivated zones.**

### Two-step modelling approach

Figures in this section show **null-model residuals** — faunal activity with the spatial grouping structure removed.

**Step 1 — Remove spatial structure.** A linear mixed model with only an intercept is fitted on the full dataset. Random effects for orientation nested within depth absorb all variation attributable to scanner placement, with an AR(1) correlation structure for temporal autocorrelation:

$$y^* \sim 1 + (1 \mid \text{orientation} / \text{depth}) + \text{AR}(1)$$

The residuals are "leftovers" on the original transformed scale, directly comparable between land-use types without spatial confounding.

**Step 2 — Test the land-use effect on residuals.**

$$\hat{\varepsilon}_{\text{Step 1}} \sim \text{land\_use}$$

β = mean(residuals at C) − mean(residuals at A). A positive β means faunal activity is higher in cultivated plots after removing orientation and depth effects.

```{r fig-h1-combined}
#| label: fig-h1-combined
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Figure 1. Direct effect of land-use type on soil invertebrate activity.**
#|   (A) Distribution of null-model residuals pooled across the monitoring
#|   period. A systematic offset from zero indicates a land-use effect after
#|   accounting for scanner orientation and depth. (B) Temporal dynamics of
#|   null-model residuals with LOESS smoothers (span = 0.18). Green = treed
#|   (A); orange = cultivated (C). Shaded bands = meteorological seasons
#|   (Spring: March–May; Summer: June–August; Autumn: September–November;
#|   Winter: December–February). Dotted red vertical lines mark key
#|   agricultural interventions in the cultivated zone (left to right: wheat
#|   harvest, sorghum sowing, sorghum mowing, tillage, chickpea sowing).
#|   Dotted green vertical lines mark irrigation events in the cultivated zone.
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ── Shared y-axis limits ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Derived from the raw data so both panels share the same scale.
# Rounded to the nearest 0.1 for clean tick positions.
shared_ylim <- c(
  floor(  min(h1_adj$adj_value, na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10,
  ceiling(max(h1_adj$adj_value, na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10
)

# ── Panel A: pooled distribution (boxplot + jitter) ───────────────────────────
# Right border suppressed so panels A and B merge visually at their shared edge.
p_box <- ggplot(
  h1_adj,
  aes(x = position_label, y = adj_value,
      fill = position_label, colour = position_label)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3,
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.05, size = 0.3, shape = 20) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outliers = FALSE,
               linewidth = 0.8, fatten = 1.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pos_colours) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = pos_colours) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Treed", "Cultivated")) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Null-model residuals") +
  guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
  theme_sem() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.border     = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.left = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x      = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin      = margin(t = 5, r = 0, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Panel B: LOESS time series ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Left border and y-axis suppressed — carried by panel A.
# Left plot margin zeroed to close the visual gap at the join.
p_temp <- ggplot(
  h1_adj %>% mutate(date = as.Date(date)),
  aes(x = date, colour = position_label, fill = position_label)
) +
  make_season_layers(
    range(as.Date(h1_adj$date), na.rm = TRUE),
    y_label_pos = shared_ylim[2]
  ) +
      # After the interventions vline:
    geom_vline(
      data = interventions, aes(xintercept = date),
      colour = "darkred", linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    # ADD THIS:
    geom_vline(
      data = irrigation, aes(xintercept = date),
      colour = "darkgreen", linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3,
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(y = adj_value), size = 0.5, alpha = 0.15, shape = 16) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = adj_value), method = "loess", formula = y ~ x,
              span = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7, se = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pos_colours, name = "Land-use type",
                      labels = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = pos_colours, name = "Land-use type",
                    labels   = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "3 months",
               expand = expansion(mult = 0.01)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6.5),
    legend.direction  = "horizontal",
    axis.title.y      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y      = element_blank(),
    panel.border      = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.right = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x       = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin       = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 0, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Assemble ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# guides = "collect" merges the two colour legends into one block placed below.
# widths = c(1, 3): temporal panel is 3× wider than the distribution panel.
h1_combined <- (p_box + p_temp) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 3), guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "horizontal")

h1_combined <- h1_combined + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
h1_combined

ggsave("../output/Fig1_H1_combined.tiff", h1_combined,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "cm", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. H2 — Indirect effects through contrasting causal pathways

> **Land-use effects are mediated through contrasting causal pathways — agricultural management drives faunal activity primarily through altered microclimate, whereas treed habitats sustain faunal activity more consistently through root resource supply.**

### Pathway algebra

The SEM estimates one standardised β per path per rolling window. An **indirect effect** is the product of all β values along a causal route. Because all continuous variables are z-scored within each window, these products are dimensionless and directly comparable across windows and pathways.

| Path                    | Expression                              |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Direct                  | $\beta_9$                               |
| lu → mc₁ → fauna        | $\beta_1 \times \beta_6$                |
| lu → mc₂ → fauna        | $\beta_2 \times \beta_7$                |
| lu → root → fauna       | $\beta_5 \times \beta_8$                |
| lu → mc₁ → root → fauna | $\beta_1 \times \beta_3 \times \beta_8$ |
| lu → mc₂ → root → fauna | $\beta_2 \times \beta_4 \times \beta_8$ |
| **Total indirect**      | sum of the five routes above            |
| **Total effect**        | direct + total indirect                 |

**Sign convention:** A = 0, C = 1. A **negative β-product means the effect through that pathway is stronger in the treed system.**

### Full-dataset piecewise SEM: path coefficient table

```{r tbl-h2-dag}
#| tbl-cap: >
#|   Path coefficients from the full-dataset piecewise SEM (all_taxa,
#|   complete time series, all depths). All continuous variables were z-scored
#|   after the calibration-selected transformation. β = unstandardised
#|   coefficient; Std β = standardised coefficient directly comparable to
#|   rolling-window values in Figures 2–3. R²m = marginal R² (fixed effects
#|   only); R²c = conditional R² (fixed + random effects). Fisher's C tests
#|   global model fit: p > 0.05 indicates that the conditional independence
#|   claims implied by the DAG are consistent with the data.

# ── Model-fit footnote string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A saturated DAG has no d-separation claims to test; Fisher's C = NA is
# therefore expected and does not indicate a problem.
fit_line <- with(dag_fit, paste0(
  "Model fit \u2014 Fisher\u2019s C = ",
  if (is.na(fishers_C)) "NA" else round(fishers_C, 2),
  " (df = ", if (is.na(df)) "NA" else df, ", p ",
  if (is.na(p_value))
    "= NA (saturated model \u2014 no independence claims to test)"
  else if (p_value < 0.001) "< 0.001"
  else paste0("= ", round(p_value, 3)),
  ") \u2502 AIC = ",
  if (is.na(AIC)) "NA" else round(AIC, 1)
))

dag_table %>%
  # Drop variance–covariance rows (~~): not directional paths.
  filter(!str_detect(response, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(
    # piecewiseSEM may store numeric columns as character — coerce first.
    across(c(estimate, std_estimate, se, t_value, p_value,
             r2_marginal, r2_conditional),
           ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))),
    across(c(estimate, std_estimate, se), ~ sprintf("%.3f", .)),
    t_fmt   = if_else(is.na(t_value), "\u2014", sprintf("%.2f", t_value)),
    p_fmt   = if_else(p_value < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p_value)),
    r2_marg = sprintf("%.3f", r2_marginal),
    r2_cond = sprintf("%.3f", r2_conditional)
  ) %>%
  select(
    "Equation"   = equation,
    "Predictor"  = predictor,
    "\u03b2"     = estimate,
    "Std \u03b2" = std_estimate,
    "SE"         = se,
    "t"          = t_fmt,
    "p"          = p_fmt,
    "sig"        = sig,
    "R\u00b2m"   = r2_marg,
    "R\u00b2c"   = r2_cond
  ) %>%
  kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE,
      align  = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "l", "r", "r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8e8e8") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = c(1, 9, 10), valign = "middle") %>%
  footnote(
    general = paste0(
      fit_line,
      "<br>Sig. codes: *** p < 0.001 &nbsp;** p < 0.01 &nbsp;* p < 0.05 &nbsp;. p < 0.10"
    ),
    general_title = "", escape = FALSE, footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

### Rolling-window vs. full-dataset pathway estimates

```{r h2-prep}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PREPARE H2 TIME SERIES
#
# Merges two sources into h2_ts (long, all pathways, community level only):
#
#   direct_ts   — land_use → fauna β from the rolling-window SEM results table.
#   indirect_ts — β-products from indirect_effects.csv, pivoted from wide
#                 (one column per pathway) to long format.
#
# Winsorisation at the 2.5–97.5th percentile per pathway is applied before
# LOESS smoothing to reduce the leverage of outlier windows (e.g. sparse fauna
# in winter) without removing those windows from the raw data entirely.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

direct_ts <- sem_results %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa", predictor == "land_use", response == "fauna") %>%
  transmute(path_id = "direct", date_start, season, std_estimate)

indirect_ts <- indirect_effects %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    c(via_mc1, via_mc2, via_root, via_mc1_root, via_mc2_root,
      total_indirect, total_effect),
    names_to  = "path_id",
    values_to = "std_estimate"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(std_estimate)) %>%
  # Derive season from date_start to be robust to older pipeline versions
  # where the season column may be absent in indirect_effects.csv.
  mutate(
    month  = month(date_start),
    season = factor(case_when(
      month %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
      month %in% 6:8  ~ "Summer",
      month %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
      TRUE            ~ "Winter"
    ), levels = season_order)
  ) %>%
  select(path_id, date_start, season, std_estimate)

h2_ts <- bind_rows(direct_ts, indirect_ts) %>%
  left_join(path_meta, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(
    path_id = factor(path_id, levels = path_meta$path_id),
    label   = factor(label,   levels = path_meta$label),
    season  = factor(season,  levels = season_order)
  ) %>%
  # Winsorise per pathway before smoothing
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  mutate(
    lo           = quantile(std_estimate, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi           = quantile(std_estimate, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    std_estimate = pmax(lo, pmin(hi, std_estimate))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# ── LOESS smoother helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#' Fit a LOESS smoother to a single pathway time series.
#'
#' Designed for use inside `group_modify()`. Falls back to raw estimates when
#' fewer than 5 valid observations are available or when LOESS throws an error.
#' `surface = "direct"` avoids extrapolation artefacts at series edges.
#'
#' @param df    Data frame with columns `date_start` (Date) and `std_estimate`.
#' @param span  LOESS bandwidth. Default 0.30.
#' @return `df` with an added `smoothed` column.
smooth_path <- function(df, span = 0.30) {
  df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(std_estimate)) %>% arrange(date_start)
  if (nrow(df) < 5) return(mutate(df, smoothed = std_estimate))
  fit <- tryCatch(
    loess(std_estimate ~ as.numeric(date_start), data = df, span = span,
          control = loess.control(surface = "direct")),
    error = \(e) NULL
  )
  df$smoothed <- if (!is.null(fit)) predict(fit) else df$std_estimate
  df
}

h2_smoothed <- h2_ts %>%
  mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)) %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  group_modify(~ smooth_path(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

date_range_h2 <- range(h2_smoothed$date_start)

# Shared y-axis limits: derived from both raw and smoothed estimates so the
# zero reference line is continuous across the panel join.
shared_ylim_h2 <- c(
  floor(  min(c(h2_ts$std_estimate, h2_smoothed$smoothed), na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10,
  ceiling(max(c(h2_ts$std_estimate, h2_smoothed$smoothed), na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10
)

# Abbreviated pathway labels for the marginal panel x-axis.
# scale_x_discrete(labels = ...) matches by break VALUE (= the full label
# string), so names of this vector must be the full label strings.
path_labels_short <- setNames(
  c("Direct", "mc1\u2192fauna", "mc2\u2192fauna", "root\u2192fauna",
    "mc1\u2192root\n\u2192fauna", "mc2\u2192root\n\u2192fauna",
    "Total\nindirect", "Total\neffect"),
  path_meta$label
)
```

```{r h2-global-coef}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EXTRACT GLOBAL-MODEL PATHWAY ESTIMATES
#
# global_coef: one row per directional path with standardised β (b) and SE.
# Variance–covariance rows (~~) are excluded. Both columns are coerced from
# character to numeric because piecewiseSEM stores them as strings.
#
# get_beta() / get_se() — safe extractors that return NA_real_ when a path is
# absent (e.g. dropped during model selection), so β-products propagate NA.
# NOTE: avoid naming these gc() / gse() — gc() shadows base::gc().
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

global_coef <- dag_table %>%
  filter(!str_detect(response, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(
    std_estimate = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(std_estimate)),
    se           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(se))
  ) %>%
  select(response, predictor, b = std_estimate, se)

get_beta <- function(resp, pred) {
  val <- global_coef$b[global_coef$response == resp &
                         global_coef$predictor == pred]
  if (length(val) == 0) NA_real_ else val[1]
}

get_se <- function(resp, pred) {
  val <- global_coef$se[global_coef$response == resp &
                          global_coef$predictor == pred]
  if (length(val) == 0) NA_real_ else val[1]
}

# Individual path coefficients and their SEs
lu_mc1      <- get_beta("microclimate_1", "land_use")
lu_mc2      <- get_beta("microclimate_2", "land_use")
mc1_root    <- get_beta("root",           "microclimate_1")
mc2_root    <- get_beta("root",           "microclimate_2")
lu_root     <- get_beta("root",           "land_use")
mc1_fau     <- get_beta("fauna",          "microclimate_1")
mc2_fau     <- get_beta("fauna",          "microclimate_2")
root_fau    <- get_beta("fauna",          "root")
lu_fau      <- get_beta("fauna",          "land_use")
se_lu_mc1   <- get_se("microclimate_1",   "land_use")
se_lu_mc2   <- get_se("microclimate_2",   "land_use")
se_mc1_root <- get_se("root",             "microclimate_1")
se_mc2_root <- get_se("root",             "microclimate_2")
se_lu_root  <- get_se("root",             "land_use")
se_mc1_fau  <- get_se("fauna",            "microclimate_1")
se_mc2_fau  <- get_se("fauna",            "microclimate_2")
se_root_fau <- get_se("fauna",            "root")
se_lu_fau   <- get_se("fauna",            "land_use")

# Global β-products and their approximate SEs.
# se_global uses the first-order product approximation (product of individual
# path SEs) — this is conservative and suitable for display error bars only;
# the exact delta-method formula is sqrt((b₂·SE₁)² + (b₁·SE₂)²).
global_paths <- tribble(
  ~path_id,         ~b_global,                                 ~se_global,
  "direct",          lu_fau,                                    se_lu_fau,
  "via_mc1",         lu_mc1  * mc1_fau,                         se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_fau,
  "via_mc2",         lu_mc2  * mc2_fau,                         se_lu_mc2  * se_mc2_fau,
  "via_root",        lu_root * root_fau,                        se_lu_root * se_root_fau,
  "via_mc1_root",    lu_mc1  * mc1_root * root_fau,             se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_root * se_root_fau,
  "via_mc2_root",    lu_mc2  * mc2_root * root_fau,             se_lu_mc2  * se_mc2_root * se_root_fau,
  "total_indirect",  (lu_mc1 * mc1_fau) + (lu_mc2 * mc2_fau),  (se_lu_mc1 * se_mc1_fau) + (se_lu_mc2 * se_mc2_fau),
  "total_effect",    lu_fau  + (lu_mc1  * mc1_fau),             se_lu_fau  + (se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_fau)
) %>%
  left_join(path_meta, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(
    path_id = factor(path_id, levels = path_meta$path_id),
    label   = factor(label,   levels = path_meta$label)
  )

# Marginal (cross-window) summaries per pathway.
# select(-any_of(...)) guards against duplicate b_global / se_global columns
# if this chunk is re-run interactively without restarting the R session.
h2_marginal <- h2_ts %>%
  group_by(path_id, label, colour, ltype) %>%
  summarise(
    median_b = median(std_estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_b     = sd(std_estimate,     na.rm = TRUE),
    q025     = quantile(std_estimate, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    q975     = quantile(std_estimate, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25      = quantile(std_estimate, 0.25,  na.rm = TRUE),
    q75      = quantile(std_estimate, 0.75,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("b_global", "se_global"))) %>%
  left_join(select(global_paths, path_id, b_global, se_global), by = "path_id")
```

```{r fig-h2-combined}
#| label: fig-h2-combined
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Causal pathway decomposition of the land-use effect on soil
#|   invertebrate activity.**
#|   (A) Marginal distribution of standardised β-products across all 144 rolling
#|   windows per causal pathway. Thick bar = interquartile range (IQR); thin bar
#|   = 95% range (2.5th–97.5th percentile, after winsorising); open circle =
#|   rolling-window median; filled circle (nudged right) = full time-series
#|   piecewise SEM estimate ± 1 SE (first-order product approximation).
#|   (B) LOESS-smoothed (span = 0.30, surface = "direct") time series of
#|   standardised β-products for the community (all_taxa). Line colour and
#|   type encode the causal route: solid = direct effect or composite pathway
#|   (total indirect / total effect); dashed = two-step indirect (lu → X →
#|   fauna); dotted = three-step indirect (lu → X → root → fauna). Shaded
#|   bands = meteorological seasons. Dotted red vertical lines mark key
#|   agricultural interventions in the cultivated zone; dotted green vertical
#|   lines mark irrigation events.
#|   Sign convention: A = 0, C = 1 — negative values indicate a stronger
#|   effect in the treed system. Both panels share the same y-axis. 
#|   Sign convention:
#|   A = 0, C = 1 — negative values indicate a stronger effect in the treed
#|   system. Both panels share the same y-axis.
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 6

shape_values <- c(median = 21, global = 21)

# Phantom data for the shape legend: coordinates are NA so nothing is drawn,
# but ggplot still emits one legend entry per shape.
legend_dummy <- tibble(
  x     = c(NA_character_, NA_character_),
  y     = c(NA_real_,      NA_real_),
  shape = c("median",      "global")
)

# ── Panel A: marginal summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The global-model diamond is nudged 0.28 units right so the two symbols are
# visually separable. Right border suppressed to merge with panel B.
p_marginal <- ggplot(h2_marginal, aes(colour = path_id, fill = path_id)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.35) +
  # Thin bar: 95% range
  geom_linerange(aes(x = label, ymin = q025, ymax = q975),
                 linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.30) +
  # Thick bar: IQR
  geom_linerange(aes(x = label, ymin = q25, ymax = q75),
                 linewidth = 2.2, alpha = 0.60) +
  # Rolling-window median (open circle)
  geom_point(aes(x = label, y = median_b, shape = "median"),
             size = 2.4, fill = "white", stroke = 1.1) +
  # Global model ± 1 SE error bar (nudged right)
  geom_linerange(aes(x    = label,
                     ymin = b_global - se_global,
                     ymax = b_global + se_global),
                 #position  = position_nudge(x = 0.28),
                 linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.35, na.rm = TRUE) +
  # Global model estimate
  geom_point(aes(x = label, y = b_global, shape = "global", fill = path_id), 
             #position = position_nudge(x = 0.28),
             size = 2.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  # Phantom layer — generates shape legend entries without drawing points
  geom_point(data    = legend_dummy,
             mapping = aes(x = x, y = y, shape = shape),
             colour  = "grey30", fill = "white",
             size = 2.2, stroke = 1.0, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = shape_values,
    breaks = c("median", "global"), 
    labels = c(median = "Rolling median (IQR / 95%)",
               global = "Global model (\u00b1 1 SE)"),
    guide  = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(
        fill   = c("white", "grey30"), # Now "white" maps to median, "grey30" to global
        colour = "grey30",
        size   = c(2.4, 2.2)           # Now 2.4 maps to median, 2.2 to global
      ))) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = path_colours, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = levels(h2_marginal$label),
                   labels  = path_labels_short) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim_h2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Standardised \u03b2") +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 5.5, angle = 40, hjust = 1,
                                      lineheight = 0.85),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 6.5),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 6.5),
    legend.key.size    = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.border       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.left   = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin        = margin(t = 5, r = 0, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Panel B: LOESS temporal time series ───────────────────────────────────────
# y-axis and left border suppressed — carried by panel A.
p_time <- ggplot(
  h2_smoothed %>% mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)),
  aes(x = date_start, y = smoothed,
      colour = path_id, linetype = path_id)
) +
  make_season_layers(date_range_h2,
                     y_label_pos = shared_ylim_h2[2] * 0.9) +
  geom_vline(
    data = interventions, aes(xintercept = date),
    colour = "darkred", linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_vline(
  data = irrigation, aes(xintercept = date),
  colour = "darkgreen", linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.6
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.65) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours, labels = path_labels,
                      name = "Pathway") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = path_ltypes, labels = path_labels,
                        name = "Pathway") +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "3 months",
               expand = expansion(mult = 0.01)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim_h2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6.5),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 6.0),
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.width   = unit(16, "pt"),
    legend.key.height  = unit(10, "pt"),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y       = element_blank(),
    panel.border       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.right  = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin        = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 0, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Assemble ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# guides = "keep" preserves each panel's own legend (shape legend under A,
# pathway legend under B). widths = c(1, 3): temporal panel 3× wider.
h2_combined <- (p_marginal + p_time) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 3), guides = "keep")

h2_combined <- h2_combined + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
h2_combined

ggsave("../output/Fig2_H2_combined.tiff", h2_combined,
       width = 24, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. H3 — Land-use forcing compresses taxonomic response diversity

> **Land-use intensification compresses taxonomic response diversity by reducing the divergence among individual taxa in their sensitivity to the land-use contrast. When the community-level land-use signal is strong — as expected under cultivated-dominant conditions — taxa are predicted to converge toward similar responses, reducing inter-taxon heterogeneity.**

### Analysis parameters

```{r h3-params}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ANALYSIS PARAMETERS — change here, propagates to all H3 chunks.
#
# pathway_focus : pathways included in the CV analysis.
#                 total_effect excluded — it compounds direct + indirect and
#                 would be redundant given that both are already shown.
# eps           : small regularisation constant added to the CV denominator to
#                 avoid division by zero in windows where all taxon β ≈ 0.
# pathway_order : panel display order.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

pathway_focus <- c(
  "direct", 
  "total_indirect",
  "via_mc1", 
  "via_mc2",
  "via_root",
  "via_mc1_root",  
  "via_mc2_root")

eps           <- 1e-4
pathway_order <- pathway_focus
```

### Data preparation

```{r h3-data}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BUILD PER-WINDOW, PER-TAXON LONG TABLE
#
# h3_direct_win   — direct land_use → fauna β from sem_results.
# h3_indirect_win — indirect β-products from indirect_effects, pivoted long.
#
# comm_beta_win   — pathway-matched community (all_taxa) β per window.
#                   Using pathway-matched rather than direct-only community β
#                   ensures the x-axis forcing proxy is ecologically meaningful
#                   for each panel (e.g. via_root forcing → via_root CV).
#
# h3_taxa_win     — individual taxa only; all_taxa excluded from CV computation.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

h3_direct_win <- sem_results %>%
  filter(predictor == "land_use", response == "fauna") %>%
  transmute(taxon, date_start = as.Date(date_start),
            path_id = "direct", std_estimate)

h3_indirect_win <- indirect_effects %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(via_mc1, via_mc2, via_root,
                  via_mc1_root, via_mc2_root,
                  total_indirect, total_effect),
    names_to  = "path_id", values_to = "std_estimate"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(std_estimate), path_id %in% pathway_focus) %>%
  mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)) %>%
  select(taxon, path_id, date_start, std_estimate)

h3_win_raw <- bind_rows(h3_direct_win, h3_indirect_win) %>%
  filter(path_id %in% pathway_focus)

# Community β per pathway × window (signed, pathway-matched)
comm_beta_win <- h3_win_raw %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa") %>%
  select(path_id, date_start, community_beta = std_estimate)

h3_taxa_win <- h3_win_raw %>%
  filter(taxon != "all_taxa")
```

### CV computation

```{r h3-cv}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMPUTE PER-WINDOW COEFFICIENT OF VARIATION (CV)
#
# For each (path_id × date_start) group:
#
#   CV = SD(β_taxa) / (mean|β_taxa| + eps)
#
# The CV is a relative dispersion measure: it quantifies how spread out
# individual taxon responses are *relative to the typical effect magnitude*.
# Because the denominator absorbs overall effect size, the CV is immune to
# the mathematical artefact that inflates raw pairwise distances in
# high-forcing windows — making it the appropriate metric for testing whether
# cultivated-dominant forcing compresses response diversity.
#
# The resulting h3_cv table has one row per (path_id × date_start), which
# eliminates within-window pseudoreplication before the downstream GLS.
#
# Outliers are removed using a 1.5 × IQR criterion applied independently
# to both CV and |community β| within each pathway, following standard
# Tukey fence practice.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#' Compute the per-window CV for one (pathway × window) slice.
#'
#' @param df  Slice with columns `taxon` and `std_estimate`.
#' @return    Single-row tibble with columns `cv` and `n_taxa`.
compute_cv <- function(df) {
  beta_vec <- df$std_estimate
  tibble(
    cv     = sd(beta_vec, na.rm = TRUE) /
               (mean(abs(beta_vec), na.rm = TRUE) + eps),
    n_taxa = length(unique(df$taxon))
  )
}

h3_cv <- h3_taxa_win %>%
  group_by(path_id, date_start) %>%
  group_modify(~ compute_cv(.x)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Attach pathway-matched community β (absolute value for GLS and dual-axis)
  left_join(comm_beta_win, by = c("path_id", "date_start")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cv), !is.na(community_beta)) %>%
  mutate(
    community_beta = abs(community_beta),
    path_id        = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_order)
  ) %>%
  # Remove outliers: 1.5 × IQR fence per pathway on both CV and |community β|
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  mutate(
    q1_cv    = quantile(cv,             0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3_cv    = quantile(cv,             0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_cv   = q3_cv - q1_cv,
    q1_beta  = quantile(community_beta, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3_beta  = quantile(community_beta, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_beta = q3_beta - q1_beta
  ) %>%
  filter(
    cv             >= (q1_cv   - 1.5 * iqr_cv)   &
    cv             <= (q3_cv   + 1.5 * iqr_cv),
    community_beta >= (q1_beta - 1.5 * iqr_beta) &
    community_beta <= (q3_beta + 1.5 * iqr_beta)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

### GLS: CV \~ \|community β\|

```{r h3-gls}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FIT GLS MODELS: CV ~ |community β|
#
# A generalised least-squares (GLS) model with a first-order autoregressive
# error structure (AR1) is fitted per pathway to account for temporal
# autocorrelation in the rolling-window series. This is the primary statistical
# test of H3: a significant negative slope indicates that stronger land-use
# forcing is associated with lower inter-taxon response diversity (convergence).
#
# Output (gls_results): one row per pathway with slope, SE, t, p, and a
# formatted annotation label for in-figure display.
# Output (annot_pos): annotation anchor coordinates (top-left of each facet).
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

gls_results <- h3_cv %>%
  filter(path_id %in% pathway_focus) %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    fit <- tryCatch(
      gls(cv ~ community_beta,
          data        = .x,
          correlation = corAR1(form = ~ as.numeric(date_start)),
          method      = "REML"),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(fit)) return(tibble())
    s <- summary(fit)$tTable
    tibble(
      slope = s["community_beta", "Value"],
      se    = s["community_beta", "Std.Error"],
      t     = s["community_beta", "t-value"],
      p     = s["community_beta", "p-value"],
      # Formatted annotation: slope ± significance stars
      label = paste0(
        "slope = ", round(s["community_beta", "Value"], 3),
        dplyr::case_when(
          s["community_beta", "p-value"] < 0.001 ~ "***",
          s["community_beta", "p-value"] < 0.01  ~ "**",
          s["community_beta", "p-value"] < 0.05  ~ "*",
          TRUE                                   ~ ""
        )
      )
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(path_meta %>% select(path_id, colour), by = "path_id")

# Annotation anchor: top-left corner of each facet
annot_pos <- h3_cv %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  summarise(
    x = min(community_beta, na.rm = TRUE),
    y = max(cv,             na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(gls_results, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_focus))  # ADD THIS
```

### Dual-axis temporal panel helper

```{r h3-dual-panel-helper}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HELPER: make_dual_panel()
#
# Builds one dual-axis temporal panel for a given pathway. Both the CV
# (coloured line, left axis) and the pathway-matched |community β| (grey
# dashed line, right axis) are shown as LOESS smoothers (span = 0.30), with
# raw CV values as faint background points.
#
# The right axis is dynamically rescaled to map |community β| onto the CV
# axis range, so both series share the same plot area without distortion.
# The horizontal dashed reference line is drawn at the CV value that
# corresponds to community β = 0 on the rescaled axis.
#
# @param pid        Character. Pathway id: "direct" or "total_indirect".
# @param show_xaxis Logical. Whether to show x-axis tick labels. Set FALSE
#                   for the top panel in a stacked layout. Default TRUE.
# @return A ggplot object.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

make_dual_panel <- function(pid, show_xaxis = TRUE) {

  dat        <- h3_cv %>% filter(path_id == pid)
  path_col   <- path_colours[[pid]]
  path_ltype <- path_ltypes[[pid]]
  path_lbl   <- path_labels[[pid]]

  # Pad axis ranges slightly so LOESS lines don't touch panel edges
  cv_rng   <- range(dat$cv,             na.rm = TRUE)
  beta_rng <- range(dat$community_beta, na.rm = TRUE)
  pad_cv   <- diff(cv_rng)   * 0.05
  pad_beta <- diff(beta_rng) * 0.05
  cv_rng   <- cv_rng   + c(-pad_cv,   pad_cv)
  beta_rng <- beta_rng + c(-pad_beta, pad_beta)

  # Linear rescaling functions: β → CV units and CV → β units
  rescale_b    <- function(x) cv_rng[1] + (x - beta_rng[1]) *
                                 diff(cv_rng) / diff(beta_rng)
  rescale_cv_b <- function(x) beta_rng[1] + (x - cv_rng[1]) *
                                 diff(beta_rng) / diff(cv_rng)

  dr <- range(dat$date_start, na.rm = TRUE)

  p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = date_start)) +
    make_season_layers(dr, y_label_pos = cv_rng[2]) +
    # Agricultural intervention landmarks
    geom_vline(
      data        = interventions,
      aes(xintercept = date),
      colour      = "darkred", linetype = "dotted",
      alpha       = 0.6, inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    #Irrigation landmarks
    geom_vline(
      data        = irrigation,
      aes(xintercept = date),
      colour      = "darkgreen", linetype = "dotted",
      alpha       = 0.6, inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    # Horizontal reference at |community β| = 0 (rescaled to CV axis)
    geom_hline(yintercept = rescale_b(0),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # Raw CV points (faint background)
    geom_point(aes(y = cv),
               alpha = 0.20, size = 0.8, colour = path_col) +
    # LOESS CV smoother (coloured, pathway linetype)
    geom_smooth(aes(y = cv),
                method = "loess", span = 0.10, se = FALSE,
                colour = path_col, linetype = path_ltype,
                linewidth = 0.8) +
    # LOESS |community β| smoother rescaled to CV axis (grey dashed)
    geom_smooth(aes(y = rescale_b(community_beta)),
                method = "loess", span = 0.10, se = FALSE,
                colour = "grey40", linetype = "solid",
                linewidth = 0.45) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      name     = paste0("CV"),
      limits   = cv_rng,
      sec.axis = sec_axis(
        transform = rescale_cv_b,
        name      = "|Community \u03b2|"
      )
    ) +
    scale_x_date(
      date_labels = "%b %Y",
      date_breaks = "3 months",
      expand      = expansion(mult = 0.01)
    ) +
    labs(x = NULL) +
    theme_sem(base_size = 8) +
    theme(
      axis.title.y       = element_text(colour = path_col, size = 9),
      axis.title.y.right = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 7),
      axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.y.right  = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 6),
      panel.border       = element_blank(),
      axis.line.y.left   = element_line(colour = path_col, linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.line.y.right  = element_line(colour = "grey35", linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
      plot.margin        = margin(t = 4, r = 5, b = 4, l = 2, unit = "pt")
    )

  # Suppress x-axis text for the top panel in stacked layouts
  if (!show_xaxis) {
    p <- p + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  } else {
    p <- p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6.5))
  }

  p
}
```

### Assemble Figure 3

```{r fig-h3-combined}
#| label: fig-h3-combined
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Figure 3. Land-use forcing compresses taxonomic response diversity.**
#|   (A) Relationship between the coefficient of variation (CV) of individual
#|   taxon β-estimates across taxa and the absolute community-level land-use
#|   coefficient (|community β|; all_taxa), shown separately for the direct
#|   (lu → fauna) and total indirect pathways. Each point represents one
#|   rolling window. The CV quantifies response diversity: low CV indicates
#|   convergence of taxon-specific responses toward a common value; high CV
#|   indicates divergence. Lines show ordinary least-squares fits; slopes were
#|   estimated by generalised least-squares (GLS) with a first-order
#|   autoregressive error structure (AR1) to account for temporal
#|   autocorrelation, and are annotated with the standardised slope and
#|   significance level (*** p < 0.001).
#|   (B–C) Temporal co-variation of the CV (coloured line; left axis) and
#|   |community β| (grey dashed line; right axis) for the direct (B) and
#|   total indirect (C) pathways. Lines are LOESS smoothers (span = 0.30)
#|   fitted to rolling-window estimates (faint points). Shaded bands =
#|   meteorological seasons (Spring: March–May; Summer: June–August;
#|   Autumn: September–November; Winter: December–February). Dotted red
#|   vertical lines mark key agricultural interventions in the cultivated
#|   zone (left to right: wheat harvest, sorghum sowing, sorghum mowing,
#|   tillage, chickpea sowing). Dotted green vertical lines mark irrigation events in the cultivated zone.
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 8

# ── Panel A: scatter CV ~ |community β| (faceted by pathway) ─────────────────
p_scatter <- ggplot(
  h3_cv %>% filter(path_id %in% pathway_focus) %>% mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_focus)),
  aes(x = community_beta, y = cv, colour = path_id)
) +
  facet_wrap(~ path_id, ncol = 1,
             labeller = as_labeller(path_labels)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method    = "lm",
    formula   = y ~ x,
    colour    = "grey20",
    fill      = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.75,
    alpha     = 0.20
  ) +
  # GLS slope + significance annotation (top-left of each facet)
  geom_text(
    data        = annot_pos,
    mapping     = aes(label = label),
    x           = Inf, 
    y           = Inf, 
    hjust       = 1.1,  
    vjust       = 1.5,              
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size        = 3, 
    colour      = "black", 
    fontface    = "italic"
  )+
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "|Community \u03b2|",
    y = "CV across taxa"
  ) +
  theme_sem(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    strip.text  = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
    axis.text   = element_text(size = 6.5),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 8, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Panels B–C: dual-axis temporal overlay (stacked) ─────────────────────────
# Top panel (direct) has x-axis labels suppressed; bottom panel (total
# indirect) shows the shared x-axis for the full figure.
p_direct   <- make_dual_panel("direct",         show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_indirect <- make_dual_panel("total_indirect", show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_via_mc1   <- make_dual_panel("via_mc1",         show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_via_mc2 <- make_dual_panel("via_mc2",         show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_via_root <- make_dual_panel("via_root",         show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_via_mc1_root   <- make_dual_panel("via_mc1_root",         show_xaxis = FALSE)
p_via_mc2_root <- make_dual_panel("via_mc2_root",         show_xaxis = TRUE)


temporal_stack <- (p_direct / p_indirect/ p_via_mc1 / p_via_mc2 /p_via_root/p_via_mc1_root/p_via_mc2_root) 
#+  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))

# ── Assemble: scatter (left, narrow) + stacked temporal panels (right) ────────
# widths = c(1, 2.5): temporal stack is 2.5× wider than the scatter.
h3_final <- (p_scatter | temporal_stack) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2.5)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")

h3_final

ggsave("../output/Fig3_H3_final.tiff", h3_final,
       width = 20, height =30, units = "cm",
       dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```
